care.summary: One of the easiest houseplants to grow. Features trailing vines with heart-shaped, often variegated leaves.
care.light: Low to bright indirect light. Variegation is more pronounced in brighter light.
care.water: Allow the top inch or two of soil to dry out between waterings. Drought tolerant.
care.humidity: Does well in average room humidity but appreciates higher humidity.
care.fertilizer: Feed every 1-2 months with a basic houseplant fertilizer.
care.toxicity: Toxic to dogs and cats; causes oral irritation if chewed.
common_issues: Loss of variegation if light is too low
tips: Easily propagated by placing stem cuttings in water.
Can be trained to climb a moss pole or allowed to trail from a hanging basket.
